headline: Model Disagreement on Market Direction Amid Jobs Report Shock
summary: Today's predictions reveal sharp divergence between Claude's bearish stance on macro headwinds and broader bullish sentiment from GPT-4o, Grok, and Perplexity. Claude cited a weak jobs report (92,000 losses) and AI regulatory concerns as recession signals, while competitors focused on pre-market momentum and positive earnings. The day remains pending evaluation as actual market closes have not been recorded.

consensus_picks.note: Three models predicted SPY upside despite Claude's bearish 78% confidence call, creating a notable 3v1 split on market direction.
Models fundamentally disagreed on tech direction with Claude bearish (65%), GPT-4o and Perplexity bullish, reflecting divergent AI regulatory outlooks.
Two models aligned on Tesla downside due to regulatory challenges and production delays, both at 60% confidence.

best_call.summary: Claude's highest-conviction bullish call on Broadcom (82% confidence) was grounded in concrete fundamentals: 28% earnings beat and 106% AI revenue surge with positive guidance.

worst_call.summary: Grok's Tesla target of $340 appears misaligned with market reality given the stock's recent valuation context, undermined by reliance on social media sentiment rather than fundamental analysis.
